text: Fast general defenses versus slower targeted memory — your immune system uses both. Innate immunity acts like a first responder, attacking invaders immediately. But did you know it has no memory? Enter adaptive immunity, which learns and remembers threats. It’s slower but packs a powerful punch. Studies show that the memory cells from adaptive immunity can last years, ready for action. Together, they form a dynamic duo, ensuring your body fights off infections efficiently. Want to learn more about your immune system? Hit that follow button!
